title: The Fermi Paradox
description: Start asking: if the universe is 13.8 billion years old and has 400 billion stars, why haven't we found any aliens? The answer might terrify you.

title: The Ship of Theseus
description: If you replace every plank of a ship, is it still the same ship? What does this mean for your body, your identity, and your self? Ancient question. No clean answer.

title: How Money Was Invented
description: Trace money from Mesopotamian clay tablets to Bitcoin. You'll understand economics, empires, and modern finance in a way no textbook teaches.

title: The Manhattan Project to Modern AI
description: How physicists built the atom bomb — and how that culture of 'big science' directly created modern computing, the internet, and AI labs.

title: The Black Death and Its Consequences
description: The plague killed 1 in 3 Europeans — and accidentally triggered the Renaissance, the Reformation, higher wages, and the modern world.

title: Why Do We Dream?
description: Science still doesn't fully know. Start here and follow the threads through neuroscience, psychology, ancient dream temples, and lucid dreaming.

title: The Invention of Writing
description: Writing was invented to track beer. Trace how marks on clay became the alphabet you're reading right now — and how it changed human consciousness forever.

title: The Many Interpretations of Quantum Mechanics
description: The science is settled. The meaning is not. Follow the Copenhagen interpretation, many-worlds, pilot wave theory, and what physicists actually argue about.
